content.problemStatement:
It's a lockdown. You’re bored in your house and are playing golf in the hallway.

The hallway has N+2N + 2N+2 tiles numbered from 000 to N+1N+1N+1 from left to right. There is a hole on tile number xxx. You hit the ball standing on tile 000. When you hit the ball, it bounces at lengths of kkk, i.e. the tiles covered by it are 0,k,2k,…0, k, 2k, \ldots0,k,2k,…, and so on until the ball passes tile N+1N+1N+1.

If the ball doesn't enter the hole in the first trial, you try again but this time standing on the tile N+1N+1N+1. When you hit the ball, it bounces at lengths of kkk, i.e. the tiles covered by it are (N+1),(N+1−k),(N+1−2k),…(N + 1), (N + 1 - k), (N + 1 - 2k), \ldots(N+1),(N+1−k),(N+1−2k),…, and so on until the ball passes tile 000.

Find if the ball will enter the hole, either in its forward journey or backward journey.

**Note:** The input and output of this problem are large, so prefer using fast input/output methods.

###Input


The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The only line of each test case contains three integers N,x,kN, x, kN,x,k.
###Output
Output in a single line, the answer, which should be "YES" if the ball enters the hole either in the forward or backward journey and "NO" if not.

You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).

###Constraints


1≤T≤1051 \leq T \leq 10^51≤T≤105
1≤x,k≤N≤1091 \leq x, k \leq N \leq 10^91≤x,k≤N≤109
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (10 points):** N≤102N \leq 10^2N≤102
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers N, x and k.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if the ball can fall into the hole either during the forward or the backward journey, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=5, k=2 the forward tiles are {0,2,4,6} and backward tiles are {6,4,2,0}. Hole at 4 is reached forward, hole at 3 and 5 are never visited.
Forward: 5%3!=0. Backward: (11-5)=6 which is divisible by 3, so the ball reaches the hole on the backward journey.
Neither 6%4 nor (8-6)=2 is divisible by 4, so the hole is never reached.

hidden.label: edge case k larger than N+1

solution.approach: The forward journey visits tiles 0, k, 2k, … . The hole at position x is reached forward iff x is a multiple of k (x % k == 0). The backward journey visits tiles N+1, N+1‑k, N+1‑2k, … . The hole is reached backward iff N+1‑x is a multiple of k ((N+1‑x) % k == 0). The answer is YES if either condition holds. Each test case is processed in O(1) time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
